steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: Which type of radiation has the greatest penetrating power?
options: All three have the same penetrating power
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Gamma radiation is the most penetrating — it requires thick lead or metres of concrete to reduce it significantly. Alpha is the least penetrating (stopped by a few cm of air or paper), and beta is intermediate (stopped by a few mm of aluminium).
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Gamma is the most penetrating. Remember: more penetrating = less ionising. Alpha is least penetrating but most ionising.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.4.3 — Nuclear radiation

steps.title: Alpha, Beta and Gamma Radiation
steps.bodyHTML: Alpha (α) radiation consists of 2 protons and 2 neutrons (a helium nucleus); it is highly ionising but stopped by a sheet of paper or a few centimetres of air. Beta (β) radiation is a fast-moving electron from the nucleus; it is moderately ionising and stopped by a few millimetres of aluminium. Gamma (γ) radiation is a high-frequency electromagnetic wave; it is weakly ionising but highly penetrating, and its intensity is only significantly reduced by thick lead or concrete.
commonErrors: Thinking more penetrating means more ionising — it is the opposite
Forgetting that beta particles are electrons emitted from the nucleus, not electron shells
Confusing gamma rays (EM waves) with alpha and beta (particles)

aqa.questionText: A detector records radiation that is stopped by a thin sheet of aluminium but not by paper. Identify the type of radiation and explain your reasoning.

steps.label: Eliminate alpha
steps.content: Alpha radiation is stopped by paper. Since this radiation passes through paper, it is NOT alpha.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correctly eliminating alpha with reasoning

steps.label: Eliminate gamma
steps.content: Gamma radiation passes through aluminium (requires thick lead to reduce it significantly). Since this radiation is stopped by aluminium, it is NOT gamma.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correctly eliminating gamma with reasoning

steps.label: Identify as beta
steps.content: Beta radiation passes through paper but is stopped by a few mm of aluminium. This matches the description.

aqa.questionText: Which type of radiation is most strongly ionising?

options.label: A
options.text: Gamma (γ) — because it is the most penetrating

options.label: B
options.text: Beta (β) — because it carries a negative charge

options.label: C
options.text: Alpha (α) — because it is large, slow and carries a +2 charge

markSchemeSteps: Alpha is the most ionising because it is a large, slow-moving particle with a +2 charge — it interacts strongly with matter ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Greater penetrating power does NOT mean greater ionising ability — the two are inversely related.
